channel.title: morningNewsLastSection
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from morningNewsLastSection, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The Most Read Books on Goodreads This Week
item.description: Here are the five most read books on Goodreads this week! These are the buzziest books of the moment, from romance to sci-fi.

item.title: Horror in the Library: Six Horror Books To Keep You Trapped in the Stacks
item.description: These books will have you looking between the stacks at your library a little bit differently.

item.description: Another week, another lineup of easy dinners to make your life a little simpler. This weekly meal plan is full…

item.description: The mystery of a missing violin, family drama in the American Midwest, a galaxy-spanning space opera, and more of today's best book deals

item.title: Meghan Trainor on Building Confidence and Healthy Habits
item.description: Meghan shares how she’s tuning out criticism, prioritizing her health, and building habits that help her feel stronger than ever.

item.title: What Happens to Your Body When You Drink Enough Water
The Magic of Having a Glass Mirror in Prison
item.description: The metal mirrors that Alabama prisons use distort our faces. When a glass one suddenly appeared, I truly saw myself for the first time in 26 years.

item.title: Does Your Library Have Its Own Annual Book Lists?
item.description: These two library-produced annual book lists showcase community interest and serve as inspiration for what other libraries can do.

item.title: 14 Book Censorship Posts to Revisit: Book Censorship News, April 24, 2026
item.description: From librarian criminalization bills to evaluating sources when reading and sharing information, catch up with these previous posts.

item.title: How North Carolina Officials Kept the Truth About a Police Shooting Hidden
item.description: In 2019, a state trooper killed Brandon Webster, claiming self-defense. Evidence contradicted that account but wasn’t made public — until now.

item.title: Ohio Bill Could Force State to Shut Down Troubled Residential Treatment Facilities
item.description: The proposal comes just months after The Marshall Project - Cleveland exposed violence at a residential youth facility northeast of Columbus.
